data.title: Highway Holdings receives Nasdaq warning over $1 share price
data.description:
• Highway Holdings (HIHO) received a notice from Nasdaq for failing the $1 minimum bid price requirement.
  • The company has 180 days, until September 14, 2026, to regain compliance by maintaining a $1+ share price for at least 10 consecutive days.
  • Highway Holdings may qualify for an additional 180-day extension if it meets other listing requirements.
  • Failure to regain compliance could lead to delisting, though the company would have the right to appeal.
  • Management may consider actions such as a reverse stock split to restore compliance.

data.title: Stock market falls as Iran lays out terms to end war
data.description:
Wall Street was deep in the red on Thursday as traders continued to monitor U.S.-Iran developments, with Tehran rejecting an American proposal to end the ongoing conflict.

The S&P 500 (SP500) -0.8%, the Dow (DJI) -0.4%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) -1.1%.

A senior political-security official has reportedly told Iranian news network Press TV that Iran has formally rejected the American proposal, insisting that Tehran will dictate the terms and timing of the war’s conclusion.

Treasury yields rose across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) climbed 3.6 basis points to 4.37%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) added 4.3 basis points to 3.94%. The 30-year yield (US30Y) edged up 2.1 basis points to 4.93%.

Fed speak features Federal Reserve Board Governor Lisa Cook, Fed Governor Stephen Miran, and Fed Governor Michael Barr.

Initial jobless claims rose in line with expectations in the past week.

data.title: Marwest REIT files for unit buyback program
data.description:
• Marwest Apartment Real Estate Investment Trust (MAR.UN:CA) announced it has filed a notice of intention to launch a Normal Course Issuer Bid.
  • The NCIB, pending approval from the TSX Venture Exchange, would allow the REIT to repurchase its own trust units.
  • If approved, up to 700,025 units (about 10% of public float) may be repurchased between April 1, 2026 and March 31, 2027.
  • All repurchased units will be cancelled after being returned to treasury.
  • The program will be executed through National Bank Capital Markets and funded using the REIT’s working capital.

data.title: Mako Mining secures Nasdaq listing, OTCQX trading to end
data.description:
• Mako Mining (MAKOF) announced approval to list its common shares on Nasdaq.
  • Trading on Nasdaq is expected to begin on March 30, 2026.
  • The company’s shares will be delisted from the OTCQX Best Market upon Nasdaq listing.

data.title: Ping An Insurance reports FY results
data.description:
• Ping An Insurance press release (PNGAY): FY Operating profit after tax attributable to shareholders of the parent company was RMB134,415 million, up 10.3% year on year.
  • Net profit attributable to shareholders of the parent company excluding non-recurring gains and losses was RMB143,773 million, up 22.5% year on year.
  • Equity attributable to shareholders of the parent company exceeded RMB1 trillion for the first time, totaling RMB1,000,419 million, up 7.7% from the beginning of 2025.
  • Ping An plans to pay a final dividend of RMB1.75 per share in cash for 2025. Total cash dividends paid for 2025 will be RMB48,891 million, having risen for 14 consecutive years.
  • Life & Health sustained business growth, increasing NBV by 29.3% year on year to RMB36,897 million.

data.title: EnerSys to shut Mexico plant, shift production to Missouri
data.description:
EnerSys (ENS) down 1.3% pre-market Thursday after saying it will close its lead-acid battery manufacturing facility in Tijuana, Mexico, and move most production to its Thin Plate Pure Lead plant in Springfield, Missouri, leveraging its investments to expand capacity within its U.S. manufacturing network.

EnerSys (ENS) said it expects to incur a ~$37M pre-tax charge under the plan, mostly to be incurred by H2 2027, including $14M as a non-cash charge primarily from equipment writeoffs; the restructuring is expected to deliver an estimated $20M annual pre-tax benefit beginning in 2028.

"The closure of our Tijuana facility and the transition of production to Springfield, Missouri, will enable us to optimize our cost structure, maximize near-term advanced manufacturing production tax benefits, and mitigate future risks associated with potential tariffs," President and CEO Shawn O'Connell.

data.title: Formula Systems GAAP EPS of $35.03, revenue of $708.38M
data.description:
• Formula Systems press release (FORTY): Q4 GAAP EPS of $35.03.
  • Revenue of $708.38M (+26.9% Y/Y).

data.title: Precigen gains after 2025 results on Papzimeos outlook
data.description:
Precigen (PGEN) shares climbed ~30% in extended trading despite reporting lower-than-expected financials as Wall Street analysts welcomed the immunotherapy developer’s outlook for Q1, boosted by its recently launched tumor therapy Papzimeos.

On Wednesday in the aftermarket, the Maryland-based biotech reported $9.7M in revenue, missing the consensus by $3.8M despite $3.4M in product revenue attributed to Papzimeos, which launched late last year in the U.S.

Meanwhile, Precigen’s (PGEN) net loss per share widened ~191% YoY to $1.37 as its selling, general, and administrative expenses surged ~70% YoY to $70.1M for the year, including $27.3M related to the Papzimeos launch.

However, citing the commercial activity so far, CEO Helen Sabzevari projected that the company’s Q1 revenue could surpass $18M, which exceeded $14.1M in the Bloomberg consensus, based on two Street forecasts.

“This is a clear sign of the enthusiasm we are seeing from patients and physicians alike, leading to a robust uptake in the therapy,” Sabzevari added, referring to Papzimeos, which the FDA greenlighted in August for a tumor condition called recurrent respiratory papillomatosis.

During the earnings call, management “guided to 1Q Papzimeos revenue exceeding $18mn, a meaningful step up from the $14mn consensus estimate," JPMorgan analyst Brian Cheng wrote with a Neutral rating on the stock. “1Q guidance positively highlights the launch momentum out of the gate.”

“Positive early launch metrics bode well for Papzimeos growth,” added H.C. Wainwright analyst Swayampakula Ramakanth with a Buy rating. “Given the broad expansion opportunities and favorable IP protection into the 2040s, we believe Papzimeos has the potential to become a franchise product," Ramakanth wrote, raising his price target to $10 from $9.

Citizens Bank analyst Jason Butler, who has a Market Perform rating on Precigen (PGEN), raised his price target to $9 from $8. “Papzimeos already stakes claim as front-line standard-of-care in RRP," Butler wrote, adding, “all key commercial metrics are impressive, in our view, including patient engagement/enrollment in hubs, reimbursement access, and advocacy.

data.title: BofA shifts European defense picks toward missile, drone tech
data.description:
Bank of America analysts are rethinking their top European defense picks, favoring companies focused on air defense systems, missiles and drones as recent conflicts highlight changing military priorities.

In a note by analyst Benjamin Heelan, the bank said it now prefers firms involved in detecting, tracking and intercepting incoming threats, rather than traditional manufacturers of tanks and artillery.

The shift benefits companies such as BAE Systems (BAESF) (BAESY), Thales (THLEF)(THLLY), Leonardo (FINMF)(FINMY), Kongsberg Gruppen (NSKFF)(KBGGY) and Hensoldt (HAGHY) (HNSDF).

Bank of America upgraded Hensoldt to a buy rating and raised price targets for BAE Systems and Kongsberg.

By contrast, the bank trimmed its outlook for more traditional defense names, lowering price targets for Rheinmetall (RNMBF)(RNMBY) and Renk (RNKGF). Sweden’s Saab (SAABF)(SAABY) was downgraded to hold, with valuation cited as a concern.

Recent conflicts in the Middle East and Ukraine have underscored the growing importance of drones, artificial intelligence and layered air defense systems for NATO countries. Analysts pointed to a recent long-range missile launch by Iran targeting a joint U.S.-U.K. base in Diego Garcia as a reminder of expanding threat ranges, even though the strike was unsuccessful.

According to the note, such developments are likely to accelerate European investment in air defense, including fighter aircraft, missile systems, radar and ground-based interception technologies.

Demand is also increasing for integrated defense platforms capable of addressing ballistic, cruise and hypersonic threats, alongside counter-drone capabilities. Companies in these segments are seeing rising interest from Gulf nations and stronger order pipelines from European governments, the analysts said.

data.title: Delivery Hero SE reports FY results
data.description:
• Delivery Hero SE (DLVHF): FY
  • Revenue of $474.3M (+30.1% Y/Y).
  • EBITDA grew 30% year-over-year (YoY) to €903 million
  • Quick Commerce GMV grew more than 30% YoY in 2025 to over €7.5 billion

  • Delivery Hero—FY 2026 Guidance

    GMV 8-10% YoY (LfL)
    Total Segment Revenue 14-16% YoY (LfL)
    Adj. EBITDA €910-960 million
    Free Cash Flow >€200 million
